examples.text: الطِّفْلُ نَامَ بِسُكُونٍ بَعْدَ يَوْمٍ طَوِيلٍ.
examples.english: The child slept peacefully after a long day.
I go to sleep or read.

senses.gloss: to abate, to subside, to let up, to calm down
to be inactive, to be listless
to neglect, to omit, to overlook
to accept, to assent to, to acquiesce in
to trust, to have confidence in

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
نام

1 نَامَتْ رِجْلُهُ (assumed tropical:) I. q. خَدِرَتْ [His leg, or foot, became benumbed, or torpid]. (TA in art. بسر.) b2: نَامَ (assumed tropical:) It (a woman's anklet) ceased to sound, by reason of the fulness of the leg: like as one says [in the contr. case], استيقظ. (TA in art. يقظ.) b3: نَامَ has for its inf. ns. نَوْمٌ and مَنَامٌ. (Msb.) b4: نَامَ الثَّوْبُ i. q. رَقَدَ [q. v.] (A, art. رقد.) b5: نَامَتِ السُّوقُ i. q. رَقَدَت [q. v.] (Th, in TA, art. رقد.) b6: نَامَ (tropical:) He lay; as opposed to he sat and he stood. See نَائِمٌ. b7: نَامَ عَنْ حَاجَتِهِ He cared not for the object of his want, neglected it. (Msb.) 2 نَوَّمَ and ↓ أَنَامَ (assumed tropical:) It benumbed, or torpified: see نَامَتْ رِجْلُهُ.3 نَاوَمْتُهُ فِى شِعَارٍ وَاحِدٍ [I slept with him in one under-garment]. (S, art. شعر.) 4 أَنْاَمَ see 2.10 اِسْتَنَامَ إِليْهِ He trusted to him, and became quiet, or easy, in mind; relied upon him. (S, K.) اِمْرَأَةٌ نَؤُومُ الضُّحَى i. q. رَقُودُ الضحى [which see]. (A, art. رقد.) نَائِمٌ (tropical:) Lying; as opposed to sitting and standing. (Mgh.) أَنْوَمُ [More, or most, prone to sleep]. (A, art. فهد.) مَنَامٌ Time of sleep. (Jel, xxxix. 43.) See also 1.
